expectations.text: Produces a structured SSP narrative format (not just general guidance)
expectations.evidence: The response is explicitly formatted as an SSP control narrative with labeled sections: 'IMPLEMENTATION STATUS' (with checkboxes), 'CONTROL DESIGNATION' (Common/Hybrid/System-Specific), 'IMPLEMENTATION DESCRIPTION', 'RESPONSIBLE ROLES' (tabular), 'EVIDENCE AND ARTIFACTS' (tabular), 'ASSESSOR NOTES', 'ORGANIZATION-DEFINED VALUES (ODVs)', and 'REVISION HISTORY'. It includes a system name, identifier, impact level, SSP version, and date fields.

expectations.text: Specifies TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 as the required protocol and notes TLS 1.0/1.1 are prohibited
expectations.evidence: Section 3.1 explicitly states: 'APPROVED: TLS 1.3 (preferred for all new deployments and where supported); TLS 1.2 (acceptable; required minimum for all in-scope communication paths)' and 'PROHIBITED — Explicitly Disabled: TLS 1.1 (disabled; deprecated per NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2, RFC 8996); TLS 1.0 (disabled; deprecated per NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2, RFC 8996).' The ODV table also confirms 'Minimum TLS protocol version: TLS 1.2' and 'Prohibited protocol versions: SSL v2, SSL v3, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1.'

expectations.text: Mentions FIPS 140-2 or FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules
expectations.evidence: Section 2.1 is titled 'FIPS 140-2 / FIPS 140-3 Validated Modules' and states: 'All cryptographic operations protecting data in transit are performed using FIPS 140-2 or FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules, consistent with the requirements of SC-13.' It provides specific module examples (OpenSSL FIPS Object Module, Microsoft Windows CNG) and references NIST CMVP certificate numbers.

expectations.text: Includes responsible roles (ISSO, system administrator, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: The 'RESPONSIBLE ROLES' section lists a table with seven roles and their responsibilities: Information System Owner (ISO), Information System Security Officer (ISSO), System/Application Administrator, Network Security Engineer, Cloud/Infrastructure Engineer, Enterprise PKI Team, and Security Operations (SOC). ISSO is explicitly identified.

expectations.text: References related controls SC-13 (cryptographic protection) and/or SC-28(1) (data at rest)
expectations.evidence: Section 6 ('RELATIONSHIP TO RELATED CONTROLS') explicitly references both: 'SC-13 (Cryptographic Protection): All algorithms and modules referenced in this narrative comply with SC-13 requirements — only FIPS-validated modules and NIST-approved algorithms are used.' And 'SC-28(1) (Protection of Information at Rest | Cryptographic Protection): Data at rest is protected separately by SC-28(1)... SC-8(1) and SC-28(1) together provide end-to-end cryptographic coverage.' The Related Controls field in the header also lists both SC-13 and SC-28(1).
